texto: Nothing vexes me so much in stupidity as the fact that it is better pleased with itself than any reason can reasonably be. It is unfortunate that wisdom forbids you to be satisfied with yourself and trust yourself, and always sends you away discontented and diffident, whereas opinionativeness and heedlessness fill their hosts with rejoicing and assurance.
explicacao: Citação de John Jeremiah Sullivan sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Nothing vexes me so much in stupidity as the fact that it is better pleased with itself than any …” articula reflexao, stupidity, yourself em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e reflexao.

texto: Sounding frank, honest, and sincere is, of course, a rhetorical strategy in itself, known from ancient literature as parrhesia. It’s often employed by liars.
explicacao: Citação de John Jeremiah Sullivan sobre temas ligados a autoestima. “Sounding frank, honest, and sincere is, of course, a rhetorical strategy in itself, known from an…” articula autoestima, liars, lying em registro de autoestima, com relevância para contextos como lying.

texto: When Lytle was born, the Wright Brothers had not yet achieved a working design. When he died, Voyager 2 was exiting the solar system. What does one do with the coexistence of those details in a lifetime’s view? It weighed on him.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de John Jeremiah Sullivan que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “When Lytle was born, the Wright Brothers had not yet achieved a working design. When he died, Voy…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, age, history, com foco em trabalho e history.
